dry_bulk.confluence_note: A sharp contrast exists between the bullish technical expansion (BDI at 99.7th percentile) and the consistently negative fundamental signals regarding Chinese industrial demand.
dry_bulk.summary: Spot rates for Capesizes have surged by 66% over 60 days despite iron ore portside inventories in China stabilizing at near-record levels. Negative steel production growth and high coal inventories suggest the current rate expansion is driven more by volatility and specific trade-lane tightness than broad industrial demand.
dry_bulk.outlook: Caution is warranted as technical momentum faces the gravity of weakening Chinese steel fundamentals and high inventories.
dry_bulk.watch: China iron ore portside inventory drawdown and Brazil export volumes.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative strength in Clean and Dirty sectors aligns with qualitative reports of unnavigable straits and geopolitical supply shocks.
tanker.summary: The tanker market has reached a state of extreme expansion, with Clean Tanker rates at the 100th percentile and Suezmax rates up significantly YoY. Geopolitical conflict in the Middle East has disrupted traditional routes, significantly increasing ton-mile demand even as spot momentum begins to cool from its initial peak. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2163.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2907.0 (ROC60 +79.6%).
tanker.outlook: Structurally bullish as long as Middle East navigation remains restricted and oil inventories continue to draw.
tanker.watch: MEG VLCC fixture activity and the status of Middle Eastern maritime bottlenecks.

macro_note: Global freight is currently bifurcated between a supply-shock driven tanker boom and a dry bulk market rallying on technical momentum despite a cooling Chinese industrial engine. The persistent $100+ oil price remains a critical cost-push factor across all vessel classes.
